THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED, AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS RULING AS FOLLOWS: FEES ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $21,277.50. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE WORK ON THE SUBPOENAS AND THE ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WAS INTERTWINED WITH THE ANTI-SLAPP ISSUES AND WAS NECESSARY FOR THE MOVING PARTIES TO SUCCESSFULLY LITIGATE THAT ISSUE. calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jul-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS LARRY YU, CARLY FONG, PHILLIP SHINN, AND THORTON, TAYLOR, BECKER & SHINN'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES

Appearances:
Lowell Haky of Charles Schwab (636-3859) for plaintiff and responding party

No other appearances. The matter was not argued, and the Court adopted its ruling as follows: Fees are awarded in the amount of $21,277.50. The court finds that the work on the subpoenas and the order to show cause was intertwined with the anti-slapp issues and was necessary for the moving parties to successfully litigate that issue. The sum of $2,975.00 is included for work in connection with the fee motion. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Peppina Thompson, CSR# 7433

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Jul-03-2008 Continued To Jul-21-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: Defendants Larry Yu, Carly Fong, Phillip Shinn, and Thorton, Taylor, Becker & Shinn's Motion for Attorney fees and expenses.

No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted in part and continued in part for further briefing. The motion is granted as to Mr. Henry's motion for fees and costs in the amount of $17,586.67. The motion is continued to Jul-21-2008, for the moving parties to address the following issues: (1) Mr. Henry's work in responding to subpoena's on Oct-29 and 31 and Nov-6, opposition to OSC on Nov-20 and CMC on Mar-17. (2) Becker and Potter to address what work is properly allocated to Mr. Shinn as contrasted with the firm per Witte v Kaufman (2006) 141 cal app 4th 1201. Shinn's fee request is denied based on Witte v Kaufman. (302/PJM/VC) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
